title: How Much Do Hair Extensions Cost?
meta_description: Complete guide to hair extension pricing, from affordable clip-ins to luxury beaded rows. Learn costs, maintenance, and quality factors.
author: Studio One
body_text:
When it comes to hair extensions, prices range from $50 for DIY clip-ins to over $4,000 for professional permanent extensions. Cost depends on installation method, hair quality, and maintenance requirements.

Clip-in extensions cost $50–$400+ and last 3–12 months with proper care. They offer flexibility without salon commitment but don't provide seamless results like professional methods.

Halo extensions range from $100–$500+ and last 6–12 months. A single weft attaches to an invisible wire. Studio One color-matches halos in-salon for perfect blending without permanent installation.

Beaded row extensions with hand-tied and Genius Wefts cost $200–$3,000+. These professional-only installations last 6–8 weeks per application with reusable wefts. They provide natural-looking, customizable results with gentle scalp care.

Hair quality significantly impacts cost and appearance. Synthetic hair is affordable but heat-sensitive. Blended hair offers moderate quality. 100% Remy human hair maintains intact cuticles, reduces tangling, and lasts longest.

Semi-permanent and permanent extensions require maintenance visits every 6–8 weeks at $50–$200 per visit. Regular appointments keep extensions secure, comfortable, and protect natural hair health while extending weft lifespan.

Studio One specializes in beaded row extensions designed to blend seamlessly while protecting hair health. Book a consultation today to achieve your dream hair.
